title: Alkermes — Relationships
html:
Alkermes Relationship Architecture

Governing Thought

The Alkermes-ZoomRx relationship is the longest-standing in the portfolio (MSA since July 2015, ~11 years) but the shallowest in terms of organizational penetration. ZoomRx is embedded as a PET/SOV research vendor to the DAI organization through two contacts (John Fritch for Lybalvi, Kurt Stauble for Vivitrol/Aristada) — reliable, recurring, but transactional. The relationship has never evolved beyond promotional effectiveness tracking into higher-value services (HCPP, ATU, strategic consulting). The upcoming CEO transition, Avadel integration, and Vivitrol generic entry create a natural inflection point: either ZoomRx expands into LUMRYZ and higher-value psychiatry research, or the account contracts as Vivitrol PET falls off and the relationship remains narrow. Sara Farina's day-to-day management has maintained stability; the question is whether there's a deliberate growth strategy for the account.

Partnership Timeline

Date Milestone
Jul 2015 Master Services Agreement signed
Pre-2021 ARISTADA SOV Tracker initiated (ongoing quarterly since)
2024 LYBALVI PET launched ($338,701 SOW) — largest Alkermes engagement
2025 VIVITROL PET launched ($117,435 SOW)
2025 SFTO proposal submitted ($89,500) — pending client decision
2025 Lybalvi PET adds Cobenfy SoV tracking — first competitive intelligence on muscarinic class
Feb 2026 Alkermes completes Avadel acquisition — LUMRYZ creates potential new research vector
Aug 2026 CEO transition: Pops → Jackson

Key statistic: ZoomRx has served Alkermes for 11 years but active SOW value is ~$456K — relatively modest for a relationship of this tenure. For comparison, J&J generates significantly more from a similar-length relationship.

Why Alkermes Keeps Working with ZoomRx

Pillar 1: PET Methodology Reliability

Alkermes values consistent, comparable promotional effectiveness data across brands and quarters. The ARISTADA SOV Tracker has run quarterly since before 2021 — a 5+ year unbroken series. This longitudinal consistency is the primary retention mechanism.

Evidence:

  • Aristada SOV Tracker has operated continuously for 5+ years across quarterly waves
  • Lybalvi PET was scoped at the most comprehensive level ($339K, 4 waves) including multichannel tracking
  • ZoomRx's standard HCP survey methodology integrates cleanly with IQVIA TPA data for prescriber targeting

Pillar 2: Competitive Intelligence Timeliness

The Lybalvi PET was expanded to track Cobenfy SoV and sample activity immediately after approval — ZoomRx provided competitive intelligence on the first new-mechanism schizophrenia drug in 35 years within the first full tracking wave.

Evidence:

  • Cobenfy reach/frequency/SoV tracking added to Lybalvi PET alongside Vraylar, Caplyta, Rexulti
  • Sample-related questions included — Alkermes is actively monitoring Cobenfy's promotional strategy
  • This is intelligence that Alkermes' internal analytics team cannot generate independently

Pillar 3: IQVIA TPA Integration

The IQVIA TPA relationship enables seamless prescriber list matching and primary market research sample construction. Sandra Maria Rodrigues operates as the bridge between Alkermes' data environment and ZoomRx's research execution.

Growth Trajectory & BD Opportunities

Vector 1: LUMRYZ / Sleep Medicine Entry

  • Opportunity: The Avadel acquisition (Feb 2026) added LUMRYZ ($315-335M) with zero current ZoomRx research. The sleep medicine commercial team is being integrated and will need market research support.
  • Approach: Map LUMRYZ team contacts through Sara Farina → propose introductory PET or HCP perception study
  • Timeline: H2 2026 (after integration stabilizes)
  • Revenue potential: $100-200K

Vector 2: Service Expansion Beyond PET

  • Opportunity: ZoomRx has only sold PET/SOV to Alkermes in 11 years. HCPP (HCP-Patient Conversations), ATU (Awareness/Trial/Usage), and strategic consulting are untapped.
  • Approach: Use SFTO proposal as a wedge into tactical optimization → demonstrate value → propose HCPP for Lybalvi bipolar I positioning or Cobenfy switch study
  • Timeline: H1 2026 (SFTO proposal pending)
  • Revenue potential: $75-150K per new service

Vector 3: Generic Transition Research (Vivitrol)

  • Opportunity: Vivitrol faces generic entry Jan 2027. Alkermes will need intelligence on brand-to-generic switching dynamics, criminal justice channel loyalty, and AUD franchise protection.
  • Approach: Propose a one-time "generic transition competitive study" as a successor to the annual Vivitrol PET
  • Timeline: H2 2026 (ahead of Jan 2027 generic entry)
  • Revenue potential: $75-100K

Rules of Engagement

  1. Sara Farina is the primary relationship manager. All strategic account decisions flow through her. Support her with competitive intelligence and BD recommendations.
  2. DAI controls budgets. Do not go around John Fritch or Kurt Stauble to brand teams. Any new service entry must be blessed by DAI first.
  3. The IQVIA TPA is critical infrastructure. Sandra Maria Rodrigues must be kept informed and happy — disruptions to TPA access would affect all three active engagements.
  4. Don't oversell during the CEO transition. August 2026 is not the time to push new proposals. Focus on delivering exceptional results on existing engagements to demonstrate value to the new leadership.
  5. LUMRYZ entry requires patience. The Avadel integration is in its first year. The commercial team is still being structured. Premature outreach could backfire.

Competitive Threats to the Relationship

Threat 1: Vendor Review Post-CEO Transition

  • New CEO (Blair Jackson) may reassess vendor relationships as part of establishing his leadership
  • Mitigation: Ensure Q1/Q2 2026 deliverables are exceptional; have Sara Farina build awareness with Jackson's office; emphasize 11-year track record

Threat 2: LUMRYZ Legacy Vendors

  • Avadel had its own market research vendors for LUMRYZ. These vendors may have deeper sleep medicine expertise and pre-existing relationships with the commercial team.
  • Mitigation: Research which vendors Avadel used; understand their strengths and weaknesses; position ZoomRx's methodology and AI capabilities as differentiated

Threat 3: Budget Reallocation to Integration

  • The $2.1B Avadel acquisition may create pressure to cut discretionary spending, including market research budgets
  • Mitigation: Position research as essential for integration success — understanding LUMRYZ's competitive position, Xywav/Xyrem dynamics, and prescriber perceptions

Threat 4: Vivitrol PET Discontinuation

  • Generic entry Jan 2027 creates a natural discontinuation point for branded Vivitrol research
  • Mitigation: Proactively propose generic transition research before the client decides to cut the engagement

Relationship Health Assessment

Dimension Rating Evidence
Longevity Strong 11-year MSA; 5+ year Aristada tracker
Revenue Moderate ~$456K active; modest for relationship tenure
Depth Weak Only PET/SOV services; no HCPP, ATU, or strategic consulting
Breadth Weak 2 contacts in DAI; no brand team or above-brand relationships
Growth trajectory Uncertain Depends on LUMRYZ entry and SFTO conversion
Churn risk Low-Medium Vivitrol PET at risk (generic); Lybalvi PET likely to renew

Key Takeaway

Alkermes is a stable but underdeveloped account. The 11-year relationship provides a foundation, but ZoomRx has never expanded beyond its initial PET/SOV beachhead. The Avadel acquisition and CEO transition represent the best opportunity in years to deepen the relationship — but only if ZoomRx acts deliberately to map LUMRYZ contacts, expand service offerings, and deliver exceptional Cobenfy competitive intelligence that demonstrates value beyond standard promotional tracking.

Accounts Hub | Home
